name: Speedy Cash
description_short: At 2812 N Main St, Fort Worth, TX 76106, Speedy Cash can be reached at +1 817-626-2774.
description_long:
Speedy Cash is a storefront lending company operating multiple locations across the Memphis, Tennessee area, including a branch at 5126 Summer Avenue. The company focuses on short-term consumer lending solutions targeting individuals who need quick access to cash between paychecks or for immediate expenses. Their Memphis location operates extended hours (9am-7pm Monday and Friday, 9am-6pm Tuesday-Thursday, 9am-4pm Saturday, closed Sunday), providing convenient access to in-person lending services.

The company offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans up to $425 with repayment due on the next pay period; Lines of Credit up to $4,000 with flexible draw and repayment terms; and Title Loans up to $4,000 secured by vehicle collateral. All loan options require standard documentation including valid government ID, proof of employment or income, working phone number, valid email, and proof of address. Title loans additionally require a drivable vehicle in reasonable condition and proof of vehicle insurance. Speedy Cash also provides ancillary financial services including money orders, wire transfers, check cashing, and Green Dot Visa debit cards.

Speceedy Cash distinguishes itself through multiple application channels—customers can apply online or visit a physical store for in-person assistance. The company emphasizes transparency and education, stating that borrowers will "know exactly how much it costs to borrow the funds prior to signing your loan agreement." Their storefront model allows customers to ask questions throughout the application process and review loan terms with trained staff. The line of credit product offers unusual flexibility for this lending category, allowing borrowers to draw funds incrementally up to their approved limit and redraw as they repay principal and interest.

However, Speedy Cash operates in the high-cost short-term lending space with limited transparency on their website regarding specific APRs, fees, or total cost of borrowing. No APR or cost information is disclosed for any product, making it difficult for consumers to compare against alternatives or understand true borrowing costs before visiting a store. The payday loan product cap of $425 suggests very short repayment terms typical of this sector, which can create debt cycles for vulnerable borrowers. While the company presents as customer-friendly, the absence of detailed pricing on their public-facing website—combined with their business model focused on short-term, high-cost lending—indicates this is best suited for genuine emergencies rather than financial planning.
address: 2812 N Main St, Fort Worth, TX 76106

company_info.headquarters: 2812 N Main St, Fort Worth, TX 76106

pros: Multiple application options: online and in-store, providing convenience for different preferences
Lines of credit up to $4,000 with flexible draw-down and redraw capability as payments are made
Extended store hours including Monday/Friday until 7pm and Saturday until 4pm for working customers
Same-day or next-day funding available through online application process
In-store staff assistance throughout application to explain terms and answer questions
Ancillary financial services (check cashing, wire transfers, money orders) available at store locations
Multiple Memphis locations (8+ stores listed) providing geographic accessibility
Clear disclosure that borrowers know exact loan cost before signing
cons: No APR or fee information disclosed on website—total cost of borrowing is completely opaque before visiting store
Payday loan cap of $425 with repayment due in approximately 2 weeks creates potential for debt cycles
Title loans require vehicle as collateral, risking loss of transportation if unable to repay
Limited alternative information or warnings about high-cost lending risks on their marketing materials
Line of credit interest and terms not detailed, making comparison shopping impossible online
best_for: Individuals facing genuine one-time emergencies (car repair, medical bill, utilities) with next paycheck arriving within 2-4 weeks
Borrowers who prefer in-person interaction and want staff guidance through the application process
People needing flexible access to larger amounts ($2,000-$4,000) via line of credit for extended situations
Vehicle owners with reliable income who can use title loans as collateral for larger loan amounts
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is best for borrowers facing genuine short-term emergencies who have reliable income and can repay within weeks, and who prefer face-to-face financial guidance. The critical caveat is that APR and total cost information are completely absent from their website—borrowers must visit in-person to learn actual costs, making it essential to compare rates with payday-alternative lenders (credit union PALs) or other emergency-cash providers before committing.
services: Green Dot Visa debit card services
Vehicle appraisal for title loan collateral
Customer service by phone (English and Spanish)
In-person loan document signing and funding
